title: Charles Research: A Genealogical Investigation
description:
A multi-month transatlantic genealogical investigation into Charles Konzen (recorded in U.S. records as Gannsen / Johnson; 1835–1903), Hessian-born sergeant of Battery B, 2nd Missouri Light Artillery, and his Trendelburg / Deisel family origins in Churhessen.

The deposit comprises the project's primary deliverables (Abstract, Methodology, Bibliography, Errata, Open Questions, Claims Dashboard, evidence cards, surname-trail analysis, and the Schräder correspondence) packaged from the canonical site at charles-research.netlify.app. The investigation follows the Board for Certification of Genealogists' Genealogical Proof Standard and uses an explicit four-state confidence taxonomy (Confirmed / Working / Open / Eliminated).

v1.1.0 (May 2026) adds the single-page Claims Dashboard consolidating every load-bearing claim with confidence pill, primary source, and falsification condition; a worked-example methodological section disentangling the five (probably six) distinct Andreas Kon(t)ze identities of the Trendelburg–Deisel records 1718–1830; a Doc A–O crosswalk anchoring the 1-day Doc-A/N death-date discrepancy; and a pre-deploy maintenance harness (canonical-claim guard + internal-link audit + rebuildable search index) that closes the propagation-cascade bug class at the build step.

Compiled by Jed Johnson with Peter Schräder of Trendelburg as German genealogist.
notes: ORCID identifiers are placeholders pending mint at orcid.org/register; the deposit metadata will be re-uploaded once both authors have iDs.
